templates.title: Post daily outreach digest
templates.description: Compile and post the daily outreach digest to the configured digest channel. Include: actions taken today, pipeline status (X leads active, X in sequence), reply rate this week, active A/B test status, and one note on what you're learning. Always post even if nothing happened (3–5 lines max). After posting, call list_tasks(assigned_to="outreach-agent", status="in_progress") and create a todo task for each lead with a touch due tomorrow (send-linkedin-dm, send-email, handle-reply, or advance-sequence as appropriate). This keeps the backlog current without re-scanning the full pipeline at next wake.

templates.title: Find leads: {topic_or_signal}
templates.description: Find 1–3 leads matching the ICP using the provided signal or topic. Try signal-based search first (Exa semantic search, Jungler post engagers, GitHub stars, LinkedIn jobs, Crunchbase funding). Fall back to direct ICP search when no signal is available. Check for duplicates against the active task pipeline before adding. For each qualified lead: create a task via create_task with title 'Outreach: [Name] @ [Company]', context containing LinkedIn URL, signal, Stage: queued, Last touch: —. Include the leads added in the daily digest.

templates.title: Enrich lead: {name} @ {company}
templates.description: Find email address and/or LinkedIn profile URL for the specified lead. Use FullEnrich if API key is in vault; otherwise try Hunter.io or Exa semantic search. Update the lead's task context with the enriched contact info. Flag if enrichment fails. Required before sending email sequences.

templates.title: Send LinkedIn connection: {name} @ {company}
templates.description: Send a LinkedIn connection request to the specified lead — no message attached (better acceptance rate). Use Heyreach if API key is in vault; use Lemlist if Heyreach unavailable; otherwise use the LinkedIn browser identity directly. After sending: update the lead's task context with Stage: connection_sent | Last touch: [today].

templates.title: Send LinkedIn DM: {name} @ {company} — touch {touch_number}
templates.description: Send the specified LinkedIn DM touch to the lead. Load the current stage from the lead's task context to determine which message in the sequence to send. Copy rules: no bullet points, no formatting, under 5 lines, pain-first opener, end with a question. Use Heyreach or Lemlist if available; otherwise use the LinkedIn browser identity. After sending: update task context with Stage: dm_[N]_sent | Last touch: [today].

templates.title: Send email: {name} @ {company} — touch {touch_number}
templates.description: Send the specified email touch to the lead using the pod's built-in email address or the configured sending domain. Subject line: curiosity or specificity, never salesy. Body: pain-first, under 5 lines, end with a question or calendar link. Use Lemlist or Heyreach for automation if available; otherwise send directly via the message tool. After sending: update task context with Stage: email_[N]_sent | Last touch: [today]. Max 10 emails per day total across all leads.

templates.title: Handle reply: {name} @ {company}
templates.description: Draft and send a reply to the inbound message from the specified lead. Apply the qualify-first framework: start with their point, stay curious, never pitch, max 40 words, end with a question or calendar link — never a dead end. Run Q1 (current approach) then Q2 (pain intensity) before proposing a meeting. Update the lead's task context with the reply summary and new qualification stage. If the reply books a meeting: complete_task(result='meeting booked | signal: [source] | date: [date]').

templates.title: Advance sequence: {name} @ {company}
templates.description: Check the current stage for the specified lead (from their task context) and send the next due touch in the sequence. Determine the correct channel (LinkedIn or email) from the task context. Follow timing rules: Touch 2 on Day 2–3 after acceptance, Touch 3 on Day 5–7 after Touch 2, etc. Update task context after sending. If all 5 touches are exhausted with no reply: complete_task(result='closed: no reply after full sequence').

templates.title: Send LinkedIn voice note: {name} @ {company}
templates.description: Record and send a LinkedIn voice note as a pattern interrupt (used in Advanced mode at Day 9 of the sequence). Draft a 15–30 second script: reference the previous touch, one specific observation, open question. Route to co-founder if voice recording is not available on this device. Update task context after sending: Stage: voice_note_sent | Last touch: [today].

templates.title: Draft cold call opener: {name} @ {company}
templates.description: Draft a 30–60 second cold call opener for the specified lead and route it to the co-founder for execution. Opener must reference the previous text touch: 'I sent you a LinkedIn message earlier this week about X'. Goal: qualify or book, not pitch. Post draft in the digest channel with a flag for the co-founder.

templates.title: Extract post engagers: {post_url}
templates.description: Use Jungler (requires API key in vault) to extract everyone who liked or commented on the provided post. Filter by ICP criteria (role, company size). Apply signal stacking for Advanced mode: only queue leads with 2+ converging signals. Create tasks for qualified leads via create_task. If Jungler is unavailable, fall back to manual LinkedIn check or Exa search on competitor post likers. Post results to digest channel.

templates.title: Update ICP: {new_criteria}
templates.description: Update the ICP definition in MEMORY.md with the provided criteria. Review the current active pipeline (list_tasks) and flag any in-progress leads that no longer match. Post a summary to the digest channel.

templates.title: Write new message variant: {angle}
templates.description: Write a new A/B variant for the opening touch using the provided angle. Replace the current losing variant in the active A/B test (the variant with fewer replies after 20+ sends). Log the new test in MEMORY.md under Active A/B Test. Rules: test one variable at a time. Highest-leverage order: opening line, first DM angle, follow-up #2 angle, CTA wording.

templates.title: Weekly outreach review
templates.description: Compute last 7 days of pipeline performance from completed and in-progress tasks: reply rate, connection acceptance rate, meetings booked, messages sent. Write the weekly learning: what worked, what didn't, one hypothesis for next week. For Advanced mode: include signal-to-meeting conversion by source and channel comparison (LinkedIn vs. email). Post to digest channel. Log in MEMORY.md under Weekly Learnings. Evaluate A/B test: kill the loser if 20+ sends on each variant, introduce a new challenger.
